reviews.text: A month ago, Courtney did her big one on my case. Courtney took my case within an eight month period  her and her team did everything that was supposed to be done  every time I have a court date, they will call me and remind me the day before they also set me up on payment plan which work perfect for me . So courtney was hired for my son’s dad to fight a case….where do i begin. She rarely returns calls. 9/10 danielle her assistant is the only one ever available to talk to. She even  forgot her client had court one day. Had we not called her office that morning she would have never shown up. She has a heavy case load and does come highly recommended but she picks and chooses what client she wants to put in the most fight for. And she is unprofessional she is always to busy to talk with her clients but she decided to respond to a social media post last night. Please do not put her on retainer or let her take your money unless she can give you some type of idea of possible outcomes. Also be ready to do the foot work. Im highly disappointed in her. reviews.text:
If I could leave zero stars, I would.

I do not know if Jodat Law Group has good attorneys because I could not even get past the rude receptionist.

I had a 3:00 PM phone appointment with Sam. My notes said he would call me, but when 3:02 PM hit and I had not gotten a call, I started questioning myself. Wanting to be respectful of their time, because if I was the one who was late, I am sure they would have told me to pound sand, I called the office to double-check.

Unfortunately, I got the same rude receptionist I dealt with the week before. She acted like helping me was beneath her.

When I mentioned my wife was also on the call with me for an uncontested divorce, she immediately got short and rude, lecturing me about how Sam could not represent both of us. I told her it was not a problem and that my wife would hang up, but she kept going on about it like I could not grasp what she was saying.

She said Sam would call me once he finished his other call. I waited 45 minutes and still heard nothing. When I manually checked my phone, I found a voicemail from Sam left at 3:02 PM, right when I was calling in. My phone never rang or gave me any missed call notification.

When I called back to explain that he must've called when I was on the phone with her she said, "There is no way," refused to believe me, and offered no apology or solution, just told me I missed my appointment.

At that point, I told her I would be using another law firm because of how rude and unprofessional she had been. She couldn't have cared less. She said, "Okay, have a nice day," and hung up on me before I could even finish speaking.

If this is the first impression Jodat Law Group wants to leave on people, good luck to them. I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere only because of the way this woman treats people.

reviews.text: Do not hire Lee White!  He wasted tens of thousands of my dollars and caused permanent damage to my child.  Even though I already had a court order giving me Full Custody, White allowed the other parent to drag out the case for 7+ years while billing me for every continuous, court prep, etc.  Multiple professionals told White that my child should not have contact with the other parent and there was a Safety Plan from law enforcement and an open criminal investigation against the other parent but White would not let them testify nor did he acknowledge their concerns.  As a result of White hiding these facts from the Court, my child was forced to see the violent other parent, and it caused irreversible harm to my child.   I had to go back to Court, pro Se, and have the Expert Witnesses testify about what my child had endured.  I won my case without White but it had a grave cost.

I had worked with Jodat Law Group on another matter, and knew what a great law firm they were. This time I needed representation in a "Trip and Fall". Attorney Jonathan Diamond was my first contact, and I was very impressed with him. Rochelle Kaufman kept my case together and moving forward and did an awesome job. My case was then turned over to Attorney CJ Dangler at Jodat Law. After one phone call with CJ, I knew I had the right attorney for the job. He spoke to me honestly, told me exactly where I stood, no fluff, just honest answers, which is what I wanted from my attorney. I knew CJ was my guy. Attorney Dangler after investigating my case, came to the determination that my injuries were due to flagrant neglect, on both the part of the condo association, where I was seriously hurt, and construction company, that the association had hired, to do work on their property, and found that their flagrant negligence that was the direct cause of my fall. On my behalf, Attorney Dangler worked very hard, going back and forth towards a settlement that would make me whole. He also made sure all my medical and hospital expenses were covered, plus ten months of physical therapy bills. I was hurt and in pain for over a year, but I knew CJ had my back. I also want to add that any time I called or emailed Attorney Dangler he got right back to me, which when your hurt like I was, was very important to me. Should you need a great attorney, Call the Jodat Law Group in Venice or Bradenton and ask For CJ Dangler. You'll be glad you did. CJ I'll be forever in your debt. Thank you, Sir.

Paola Stay is an exceptional family law attorney. She is extremely knowledgeable, compassionate, and truly understanding of my specific situation. She took the time to clearly explain every step of the process so I always understood what was happening, and she carefully walked me through all of our options with a thoughtful and strategic approach.
Paola and her entire team went above and beyond my expectations. I never had a phone call go unanswered or an email left without a prompt response. She was thorough, detail-oriented, and proactive, which made me feel fully prepared and confident going into every hearing. The outcome of my case was far better than I ever expected.
I am incredibly grateful for her dedication and highly recommend Paola Stay and her team to anyone in need of outstanding family law representation.

I cannot recommend Paola Stay highly enough. From our very first meeting, it was clear that she genuinely cares about her clients and understand how emotionally challenging family law matters can be. She was compassionate, patient, and incredibly knowledgeable, taking the time to explain every step of the process in a way that made me feel informed and confident.

Paola was always responsive, professional, and strategic, while still keeping my family’s best interests at the center of everything. I felt supported and advocated for throughout the entire case, and the outcome exceeded my expectations.

If you are looking for a family attorney who is trustworthy, dedicated, and truly exceptional at what they do, look no further. I am deeply grateful for her guidance and expertise

Mrs.Paola was truly amazing, listened to my needs and concerns, treating me with respect and empathy, not just like another case number. She and her team took the time to understand the nuances of my situation and my primary goal and the best interest of me and my twins! I never had an unanswered email or question. The entire firm was incredibly prompt with responses, keeping me informed every step of the way. This constant communication made the process much less stressful and easy. I’m very thankful! If I could give more than 5 stars I would in a heartbeat. Thanks again Paola!
Very caring and honest. Highly recommend!

Donna and her staff were so knowledgeable and helpful after my mom passed away.  My mom made me executor and trustee which seemed like insurmountable tasks at first as I had a lot to learn.  With the help of Donna and her experienced staff, however, they laid out the process and made it seem relatively simple... at what I considered a VERY reasonable price!  Well worth it to get that kind of help!  I have no affiliation with Donna or anyone at her office: met them for the first time when I went for help.

If GOD or the Fates should put Donna Irvin Sobel in your path, then consider yourself blessed and fortunate.
If it is Estate planning beforehand(and this is what you need to do)or afterwards(you do NOT want this additional burden on your grieving  loved ones)then the expertise of Donna Irvin Sobel Law Office is what you need.
She and her staff, Anita Falls, Medicade expert, and Sharon, Probate expert know the in's and out's of Florida's Elder Laws.
They are a great team to have assisting and guiding you.

And, you might ask, why I think so highly of this Law Office? I'm the grieving one left to straighten out(truth be told, Donna did all the work)family Estates, twice!
So happy DONNA was there to help me thru the maze of Elder law when my Mom passed.

Clearly very bright, but will not interact by email (must go thru paralegal).  Felt pressured, rushed...wanted to fit everything into her template and did not consider our personal situation.  When we suggested approach used by other attorney, didn't appear to want to work to develop relevant issues.. Argued her points well, but we still did not g=feel comfortable. Price for package approx $1000 higher than quotes competing elder law attorneys.
Great personality
Excellent service, with elder law.

Amazing is the only word I would use to describe Donna. She has prepared Wills, Durable Power of Attorney, and Trusts not only for my parents but for my in laws as well. My Father in Law was a practicing Attorney for over 30 years and he was extremely pleased. Donna brings a rare combination of knowledge, compassion and trust. During one of conversations she asked if I might be interested in adopting a dog, the same breed as mine, just in case one of her older clients wasn't capable. That says it all. Thank you Donna.

Employee signed agreement to pay office back over 18 months.  They sent payments weekly for 18 months until all was paid back.

We got all of the money back. and think about what your really want....
What good in justice if you don't get the money back.

I have used Mr. Meeks to get contract laborer's to live up to the work they promised.
He helped avoid big legal issue and get my work done without bad feelings with the contractors.

He is a rare attorney that wants to settle with the least cost and legal involvement.

Some attorneys try and stir the pot .....he tries to turn off the heat and serve both parties with a bowl of chicken soup and avoid heartburn of legal confrontation.

Dr. Ed Fliss

This is the second time Ive had to use the services of Adam and Aaron at Harllee and Bald. Both times, we achieved a positive and successful outcome. They were always informative and always just a quick phone call away! Adam was very professional, upfront, and honest from the start. Adam took the time to understand the intricacies of my situation, providing clear explanations and guidance every step of the way. His attention to detail and commitment to achieving the best possible outcome were evident throughout the entire process.
Not only does Adam possess an impressive depth of legal knowledge, but he also exhibited excellent communication skills, keeping me informed and always updated. I felt that he fought hard for me and was reassured knowing that he always had my best interest in mind. If you find yourself in need of an attorney I would absolutely recommend Adam!

I had a fantastic experience with Harllee & Bald, P.A. This law office impressed me with their professionalism and dedication.
I'd like to highlight Adam Mohammadbhoy's exceptional work. His expertise, communication, and hard work  was outstanding. He ensured I was well-informed throughout, and his efforts led to a positive outcome.

I hope that we do not have to go through something like this again, but if we do, I would certainly look for Aaron to help us through!

My husband and I had a wonderful experience with the Harllee & Bald firm.  They obtained the results we wanted in a property dispute and in a very timely manner.  Our attorney, Aaron Crittenden, took the time to make sure all of our questions were answered. I would definitely use Harllee & Bald again.

I had a wonderful experience with Aaron Crittenden. I haven’t yet had to hire him but if I need a lawyer it will be him! Not only did he take the time to speak with me and learn about the situation, he reviewed my contract and emails, called me back in a timely fashion, and gave me actual legal advice without a payment. I called around as I am a new business owner and wanted to make sure that I got someone that understood my business and cared. Aaron is definitely that person. I felt like he would work hard to win for me. If I need him, I know he will. So glad I reached out! He is reasonably priced in comparison to some others that wouldn’t even speak to me without a retainer twice the price that Aaron mentioned to me over the phone. If I’m going to court, it’s with Aaron!!
Aaron has been great for a not so great situation. Completely recommend

We hired Harllee & Bald to help us with some very complex and high stakes vexatious litigation. Kim Bald was the lead attorney with Adam as he second chair, but most if not all of the lawyers got involved to one degree or another.

First let me say this....I am an attorney myself and am no stranger to the law and lawyers.  I can say without fear of contradiction that Kim is the finest attorney I ever worked with, here in Florida or in Big cities like NY.

She knows her stuff, she handles herself well, she takes no guff from annoying and unprofessional opposing counsel and most important, she always comes prepared for anything.

She has terrific bedside manner and is extremely practical.  I know that in her heart and mine, we had a winning case and that it would likely be a good fight and almost surely a win.  But she was wise enough to guide us to a smallish settlement while forgoing the fun of the fight and the fees she’d earn.

Adam is a stellar lawyer - he’s had great experience and it’s given him tremendous judgement.  He too has the right touch with clients and I can’t recommend him more highly.  A fine lawyer and a very decent down to earth guy.

Kimberly, Adam and their team were a pleasure to work with and helped us secure a favorable outcome to our case, despite the tough reputation/track record of the defendants. They were consistently on top of the details and deadlines, broke down complex legal information in ways that were easy to understand, provided sound counsel and made sure to keep us on task! Highly recommended.

I hired James E. Lynch after interviewing several other attorneys on my father's auto accident case.  Hiring an attorney wasn't new to me, I dealt with multiple attorney's on business, insurance, divorce and other related cases.
Working with James was unlike my previous experiences, he was always on point, resourceful and prompt at prioritizing and resolving different parts that made up the case.
My fathers' case was favorably resolved and the process to get there was handled by James in the  most professional  manner.
Anyone looking for personal injury attorney  in Bradenton, FL area should start their search with James E. Lynch.

Mr Crittenden made a horrible experience enjoyable with his professionalism, expert knowledge, and tactful way of handling a fraudulent litigation.  Words cannot express our gratitude to Harllee & Bald for getting us through our legal battle.

My experience with the law firm Harllee and Bald was extremely positive. James Lynch and his staff provided the necessary guidance and expertise for the handling of my brother's complicated estate. I would highly recommend Harllee and Bald for any form of litigation.

reviews.text: When my auto was struck by a speeding motorist, who ran a red light, causing me to sustain 8 fractures and my wife 9 fractures, I contacted Mr. Shapiro when I was subsequently discharged from the hospital. He very professionally represented us both for recovery of damages for our injuries. I myself had practiced law for almost 40 years in upstate New York, before retiring to Sarasota Florida. I am quite familiar with these type of matters, but I chose to not handle this myself, mainly because I had met Mr. Shapiro previously and was very impressed with his demeanor and obvious knowledge of these type of matters. He immediately opened a file on our case and handled it admirably. It took almost a year for the case to be settled, mainly because he had to determine the extent and prognosis for both me and my wife. When the doctors finally discharged us, Mr Shapiro went to work and obtained for us a very favorable settlement, without having to go to court. We had several meetings, and I could easily see that he had all the bases covered. He kept us informed as the matter progressed and handled it so that there were no loose ends to be taken care of by us. We know of several others that Mr. Shapiro represented and they gave us a very high recommendation, which is why we obtained services. We are glad that we did. I would not hesitate to recommend him to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Donna! We appreciate the kind words!
We appreciate the five stars, Christopher!

I was rear-ended in 2019 and going to James & his team was the best decision of my life (literally). I didn’t think I’d need an attorney since my car wasn’t in bad shape, but after months of neck pain, we later found out I had a tear in my shoulder and I would need surgery because of this accident. I heard about James through a coworker and was immediately comfortable and put at ease about what the process would like after just one meeting. From the second I chose them, they took care of all the insurance mess, taking an immense amount of pressure off of my shoulders (pun intended). They made sure I saw the best medical providers around with little to no waiting period, and never having to deal with the insurance/money back and forth nonsense. This whole time I was in pain and learning I would need surgery, I was working full-time and was in my last semester of University, so they helped in ways I will always be grateful for!

On top of everything, I am a violinist of 14 years, no longer able to play my instrument because of this accident. When the surgeon gave me the choice of dealing with the pain and not really ever playing my violin comfortably, or surgery with a 6+ month recovery time, I was a mess. James’ staff, specifically Jen, was on the phone with me helping me weigh the options and truly helped me through that very emotional decision. Now a year after my surgery, I am back at playing my violin, with James and Jen having taken care of the case for me. I will forever be grateful for the work they did for me and the personable experience I know I would not have gotten anywhere else. I highly, highly recommend!!!!

Then they will say…you have to call again because the attorneys can’t call you. What law firm has attorneys who are not allowed to call their client?  Such BS. While we had a positive result in the end with a Trust, Pour Over Will, and PoAs, we had several problems along the way such as:
1) We received a Trust, Will, Living Will document drafts with incorrect Executor information. Not what we had discussed in our initial meeting.
2) One Beneficiary name (my daughter’s middle initial) was incorrect stated.
3) The warranty deed which places the house into the Trust was missing from the draft documents sent. My wife was familiar with this process via her father’s Trust but a less familiar client might setup a Trust with no assets in that Trust. Not helpful.
4) Our insurance company wanted us to upload a copy of the Trust since our Trust now owned the Home. However, we just received a read-only, encrypted version that could not be uploaded to our insurance company. We asked for an unencrypted version but Affordable Attorney would not send us an unencrypted document.  So we had to pay for 40+ pages to be scanned and converted to a PDF. Not helpful.
>>Overall, the handoff between our lawyer and the paralegal who prepared the documents was flawed. I question whether documents prepared for other clients are flawed, as well. But thankfully we knew what to look for in this process or we might have had a horrible result.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience and concerns. Through the estate planning process, we make sure to include opportunities for clients to review drafts of their documents and for the attorney to go over the documents with the clients. During the review process, revisions are often needed. These revisions can result from incorrect information provided to our office, misunderstanding during verbal discussion of the plan, and clients reconsidering aspects of the plan. Both at the initial appointment and at the end of the representation, we also advise clients regarding their specific assets and how to title or otherwise designate each asset in light of the client’s plan. By the end of the process, we want to ensure clients have documents that are accurate, properly implement their wishes and are protected from challenge. The review time and asset discussions address the first two and our policies regarding confidentiality address the last. Confidentiality is essential, particularly to protect the plan, and we have strict policies regarding not disclosing client’s information to anyone other than the client and measures designed to provide protection of the documents themselves. The client receives all their original documents, along with copies, and the client themselves may choose to share aspects of their plan with trusted family members or advisors; however we would encourage serious consideration before sharing unprotected details with others. We strive to give clients confidence in their plan and are sorry that your experience through the process raised concerns, but we are glad that your final documents did meet your estate planning goals.

I cannot say enough about Caleb and Jen Hensley and the team at Hensley Law.

The night before I was scheduled to be induced, I went into a panic realizing I had not completed critical documents, my living will, advanced directives, and other paperwork in case anything happened during labor. Without hesitation, Caleb and Jen drove over an hour from their home to mine on Siesta Key, late in the evening, to make sure everything was handled properly and with care.

They sat with me in my home, walked me through every document with patience and compassion, and completed everything thoroughly and professionally. This was not just legal work, it was an act of genuine kindness and humanity during one of the most vulnerable moments of my life.

Their willingness to show up, personally and selflessly, speaks volumes about who they are and how they practice law. They went far above and beyond anything I could have expected, simply because they care that deeply about the people they serve.

Caleb and Jen are exceptional professionals, but even more incredible people. I will forever be grateful for their generosity, their calm presence, and the peace of mind they gave me when I needed it most. If you are looking for attorneys who lead with integrity, heart, and true service, Hensley Law is it.

Being able to bring you peace of mind when you needed it most is something we do not take lightly. We are grateful that we could be there for you, not just as attorneys, but as people who genuinely care. reviews.text: One star is being generous for this firm. I had a consultation with Jen, reviewed the details of my case and she offered to take my case on a contingency basis. After 6 months with no communication I called the office and was told “sorry we are behind schedule but will be in touch soon”. Another 6 months passed and I called and was told the exact same thing. Another 6 months passed, I called and finally they produced a demand letter to send. The letter was factually inaccurate and content was elementary at best. After several back and forth with attorney named Myanda, the corrections I requested were not made properly and letter was sent with the defendants name misspelled. At that point they sent me a client agreement that would no longer represent me on a contingency basis and would require a retainer to move forward. After a year and half of screwing me around they offered no explanation or apology for the subpar legal representation. I give 0 stars!!!

reviews.text:
I wish I could give this firm zero stars. My experience was incredibly frustrating, and I feel it’s essential to share my concerns to help others avoid similar issues.

After weeks of diligently following their advice and waiting for essential guidance on my rent-to-own contract, I was abruptly informed that they could no longer represent me due to a “breakdown in communication” and my supposed failure to follow their legal advice. This was utterly shocking, especially given the significant amount of time wasted in unproductive back-and-forth discussions that yielded no results. What’s even more absurd is their claim in the termination letter that I withheld information from them. They had all the necessary documentation and paperwork related to my contract and situation, which makes their accusations completely unfounded.

Communication was consistently inadequate. Myanda frequently failed to return calls as promised, and I often found myself waiting a week or more for a response. Timely answers to my questions were hard to come by, and it felt as though my concerns were disregarded. Instead of offering clear guidance, I was left in the dark, which ultimately led to a substantial loss of time and momentum in my case.

If you value effective communication and professionalism, I strongly advise you to stay away from them. Their lack of responsiveness and accountability makes it incredibly challenging to navigate legal matters. Save yourself the frustration and seek representation elsewhere.

I would like to express my sincere gratitude to the Center for U.S. Immigration Services for the exceptional assistance and support provided throughout the visa application process. In particular, I want to highlight the outstanding work of Attorney Yohan A. Zingile, who was instrumental at every stage of the process.

From the very beginning of my journey with the Center, the experience has been nothing short of exemplary. The immigration process can be complex and overwhelming, but the team at the Center did everything possible to make it as straightforward and transparent as possible. However, what truly made a difference was Attorney Yohan A. Zingile.

Attorney Zingile demonstrated a level of professionalism and dedication that exceeded all my expectations. He was involved in every step of the process, providing clear and precise guidance at each turn. His deep knowledge of immigration law and his ability to communicate complex details in an understandable manner were invaluable.

Furthermore, Yohan A. Zingile stood out for his meticulous attention to detail and his ability to anticipate and address potential issues before they arose. His proactive approach and constant commitment made me feel supported throughout the entire process. His approachable demeanor and willingness to answer all my questions, no matter how minor, reflect his genuine dedication to his clients' success.

Thanks to Attorney Zingile’s efforts and professionalism, my visa application process was carried out efficiently and without any hitches. His work has been crucial in reaching this important milestone in my life, and I could not be more grateful for his support and dedication.

I highly recommend the Center for U.S. Immigration Services and, in particular, Attorney Yohan A. Zingile to anyone in need of immigration assistance. Their excellence and professionalism are truly commendable and have made a significant difference in my experience. Thank you so much for everything!

Any ordeal that transpires over a 3 year period can be difficult.  However, that is not the case with Kimberly Cook and Mike Sexton as they took on my daughter's case of applying for disability through Social Security.  Once I understood the paperwork they needed from a myriad of doctors, therapists, former employers and psychiatrists, I was able to go to work and compile what they needed.  If any piece of information was missing or needed further explanation, Ms. Cook and Mr. Sexton were there to walk me through it and together we went to work on obtaining it.  Kimberly was always available and willing to (cheerfully) give me an update on the progress.  I never felt as if I was bothering her and she made me feel as if my daughter's case was of high importance to her -- always having the details at her fingertips.  When my daughter failed to show for her first hearing, Mike handled it with ease and grace assuring me that sometimes this happens with clients suffering a mental illness and we would continue supporting her needs in this effort.  However this case turns out, I have full faith and confidence that Avard and these two individuals specifically gave it 150%.  Thank you for giving me the opportunity to let you know.

I would highly recommend this firm!

I contacted Daphane Stevens to be the Attorney for my Divorce in July 2024. I paid her a substantial retainer for the process (6k). She said she would “Fast Track” the paperwork.
On subsequent appointments to her office I realized how disorganized and unprofessional she was. She replaced her Paralegal and receptionist after 2 months.  New people who weren’t able to answer questions. I arrived to a 1:00 appointment with the Paralegal, who didn’t come in that day and they hadn’t bothered to call me. Daphane was there and acted like she didn’t know me or what my case was about. Told me “I have other cases to take care of”. By October, my X and I decided to go “No Contest” which meant no mediation or Court appearances. Meaning Attorney services were almost nil. I repeatedly had to remind her of the terms we had agreed upon. Finally after 5 months she did the paperwork to finalize the Divorce. She told me that she spent 20 hours in preparation. I doubt that she even spent 10 hours. In March 2025, she sent me a bill for $10.50 due to the Circuit Court for the final filing, dated 12/19/24 that had  been sent to her. Kyle and his staff worked hard and diligent to resolve my final marital agreement. I couldn't have been more pleased with their work and dedication.

Kyle is a shark—he doesn’t play any games and is incredibly responsive, always keeping you updated on his progress. He’s not intimidated by big companies and possesses exceptional intelligence and strategic thinking. He’s one of the most honest lawyers I’ve ever encountered.

From day one, Kyle was an absolute game-changer in my divorce, and I genuinely can’t recommend him enough. He’s incredibly responsive - every call, email, or off-the-wall question gets a quick, clear answer, which makes a world of difference when everything feels overwhelming. His billing is refreshingly fair and transparent; he itemizes everything down to the minute, so you always know exactly what you’re paying for and why.

What I value most is his steadiness and deep knowledge. He guides you through the process in a way that actually makes sense, and he’s fantastic at preventing things from spiraling or becoming more dramatic than they need to be. Kyle quietly handles so much behind the scenes to keep the stress off your shoulders, and he only brings you what truly matters. And when the moment calls for it - he will fight like crazy to protect you.

If you want an attorney who’s exceptionally skilled, genuinely invested in your well-being, and ready to stand up for you when it counts, Kyle is absolutely the one you want in your corner.

He was very nice to me and he got me everything I set out to get help with and some! He is very smart and takes his time to know his customer. I would suggest him to everyone going through child support because he shows compassion for you as you go through a sad time and the kids. I felt like he actually wanted the best for us. Chose him guys you can’t go wrong !  Thanks so much Kyle you helped us . God bless you

Kyle and his team are amazing, they handle my car accident case in a timely manner, keeping my husband and myself aware of everything that was going on. I have referred 2 of my coworkers to him after their car accident. I will definitely use Kyle and his team again. Thanks for all your hard work and dedication to my family.

Kyle Stalnaker is an exceptional attorney with a unique combination of empathy, legal knowledge and skill, family values, and competitiveness. After speaking with other divorce attorneys, I was feeling a bit hopeless. Kyle made me feel heard and optimistic. Although my case had its challenges, Kyle helped me develop realistic expectations based on case law and his vast experience. Once negotiations began he excelled at strategy, communication, and organization. He methodically advocated for me and when negotiations began to stall, he fought hard for my essential requests. I am so grateful to Kyle and his amazing staff for helping me and my family through this stressful process.

Kyle is a true ninja in the legal world.  He's calm, strategic, and always working behind the scenes to put me in the best position possible. From the very beginning, he made me feel heard and understood, not just as a client, but as a dad trying to protect his kids. He genuinely cared about us and it showed in everything he did. I always knew he was in my corner, fighting smart and never wasting a move or money. Because of his dedication, and the incredible support of his team, we came out on top. I’ll always be grateful for the way he stood by me and my family during one of the hardest times of my life. If you want someone who will fight for you like it’s personal to them, Kyle is that person.

I have ZERO tolerance for a god complex, and I generally prefer to work with women—but a friend recommended Kyle and promised me I would like him and that he wasn’t like that. I’m happy to say she was right. Kyle was kind, understanding, and easy to work with throughout the entire process.

He never pushed his own agenda. In fact, he did everything he could to encourage me to use what leverage I had to get my ex to cooperate so we could avoid court. That route meant far less income for him, but he genuinely prioritized what was best for me.

He didn’t just tell me what I wanted to hear—he was honest and direct, and spoke from his experience (but in a kind way) so I could make truly informed decisions.

He also wrote up a very thorough settlement agreement and thought of things that hadn’t even crossed my mind. I feel like I’m as protected as legally possible as I move forward.

If you’re looking for someone grounded, competent, kind, and human—Kyle is all of the above, which is priceless in something as difficult and emotional as an unexpected divorce.

If you find yourself navigating any aspect of family law, your first step should be getting in contact with Kyle and his extraordinary team.

I was fortunate to receive his support and guidance while navigating a divorce that was both unexpected and complicated by the challenges created by working overseas. Throughout the entire process, Kyle went far beyond the scope of a traditional client service relationship and delivered exceptional communication despite a 12-hour time difference.

There were many times when my desire to seek an amicable resolution were thwarted by the other party’s expectations, greed, and stalling tactics. These desires were often driven by a sense of guilt and accountability for my role in the divorce and Kyle perfectly balanced protecting my best interests and advocating for me while staying true to my principles and values.

Kyle is authentic, compassionate, and empathetic. He is realistic and fair. He is also a brawler. He was originally recommended to me for his reputation as a bulldog. He will fight for his clients and use his talent, experience, and extensive network to maximum advantage whenever necessary. Like any good bulldog, he fights only when he must and he is loyal to his people to a fault.

Kyle is an incredible person and — in a competitive world — a winner. Getting to know him was one of the few bright spots of an otherwise exhausting and demoralizing process. The cost of doing what is right and the pursuit of happiness can be daunting and extremely challenging. Completing the divorce process and benefiting from the help of such an incredible person were the rewards and I am grateful beyond words for them both.

Kyle Stalnaker is the only person I would recommend to my friends and colleagues to help them navigate the emotional minefield of any family law-related matter. If you’ve read this review, you’ve found the right person.

If you’re going through a divorce, amicable or high-conflict, Kyle is the attorney to hire!

Before choosing an attorney, I met with several lawyers, but Kyle immediately stood out. From the moment I sat down with him, I felt more at ease. He gave me a great vibe, calm, kind, confident and very knowledgeable. He shared more helpful, practical information in our first meeting than any of the other lawyers I spoke with. He was also highly recommended in local Facebook groups, and now that I’ve worked with him, I can confidently say: the praise is well-deserved.

I was navigating an amicable divorce, but I wanted an attorney who would move quickly, made sure everything was done correctly, and be ready to protect me if anything changed. Kyle is exactly that balance. He’s incredibly compassionate, approachable, and responsive, but don’t let his kindness fool you. He’s also a shark when he needs to be. He understands strategy, he’s detail-oriented, and he won’t let anything slide that could put you at risk. He knows how to keep things peaceful when possible, but he also knows how to fight if the situation calls for it.

What makes Kyle truly exceptional is his ability to tailor his approach to your situation. If you’re looking for a lawyer who can help you navigate a respectful, cooperative divorce, he will support you with care and efficiency. If you’re facing a more contentious or complicated split, he will protect your interests fiercely. Either way, you’ll feel heard, supported, and informed every step of the way.

There were several complexities in my case, and Kyle handled every one of them with clarity and professionalism. He explained things in a way that made sense, always kept me updated, and never once made me feel rushed or overwhelmed. I felt completely safe in his hands.

The best part? He genuinely cares. That’s rare in this field. Kyle takes his role seriously, not just as a lawyer, but as someone who is helping people through one of the hardest transitions of their lives.

He’s the best attorney I’ve ever worked with, and I recommend him wholeheartedly. Whether you’re hoping for an easy split or preparing for a legal battle, Kyle will show up for you in all the right ways.
I highly recommend Kyle.  He and his staff were extremely helpful handling a prenuptial agreement.  Great care and communication.

While looking for divorce attorney referrals on multiple local Facebook groups, I continued to come across one highly referred name..Kyle Stalnaker. I can attest that he lives up to the hype. reviews.text:
Avoid at all costs unless you enjoy doing your own legal work.

If you are looking for an attorney who will disappear the moment your retainer clears, Mackey is your man. He is full of empty promises during the initial consultation, but once he has your money, the "service" ends.

Here is what you can actually expect:

Zero Communication: They will ignore your emails and calls for weeks, only to resurface when it's time for another retainer.

Disorganization: He showed up to my session with a massive file of paperwork that wasn't even mine. It's clear they don't look at the file until they are walking through the door.

You Are the Paralegal: We ended up doing the majority of the paperwork for on our own family case. He didn't even bother to review the parenting plan to advise on what was in our best interest.

Billing is a Joke: The fees are astronomical for the lack of effort provided. By the third retainer, you realize you're just funding his lifestyle while he has side conversations with opposing counsel to keep the case dragged out for his own benefit.

Total lack of ethics. He will even have people from the office who know nothing about your case "handle" things just to keep the clock ticking.

Do yourself a favor: get every single promise in writing during the consult, or better yet, find a lawyer who actually cares about your family. He dropped my case after making a formal complaint I will have no choice but to take this up with the bar with receipts of all of the delays and mishandling of billing and casework.
reviews.ownerResponse:
As to Creative Co.'s complaint, I have traced through and found that it is allegedly a fictitious name for a dissolved Florida entity called ___ & ___ Designers Inc. The principals are apparently parents of an ex-client who we concluded representing in 2023.

The daughter came to us in 2023 after she had already been through 2 other lawyers, we were her third lawyers. The client signed an engagement letter and placed the advance fee/retainer with our firm. The client then never made any other payment and ended up owning our law firm $4016.49.

Our representation accomplished exactly what the client wanted: we negotiated and prepared a long distance parenting plan. Our client signed the plan and our client’s ex also signed the plan. The court then approved the plan, and we also procured a child support amount to be paid by the client’s ex.

It was only after the client had resolved the case and when we asked for payment of the remaining amount due, that the client started to complain about our representation. This complaint, two years after the representation was concluded is inaccurate and baseless.

Peter Mackey, Esq.

reviews.text: If you want a lawyer who will drop you as a client a couple of weeks before trial, after spending thousands, Jose Estigarribia is your go to.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Alvarado’s case was proceeding toward trial.  However, I continually had to advise Ms. Alvarado that our law firm’s engagement agreement, which she had signed, required her to maintain a retainer at a certain level. Ms. Alvarado was not complying with the retainer portion of our law firm’s engagement agreement. During the case, I even allowed Ms. Alvarado to make payments, so that the legal representation could continue. Unfortunately, as we came up to trial, Ms. Alvarado could not keep up with her contractual payment obligations, specifically the retainer requirement, and my firm had to withdraw from representing Ms. Alvarado. Otherwise, if I had not withdrawn, I would have been “working for free”. We had a hearing before the Judge, Ms. Alvarado was present, and I was allowed to withdraw from representing Ms. Alvarado. She then chose to proceed to trial with no lawyer. It appears that the no-lawyer decision was not prudent and Ms. Alvarado now wants to unjustifiably blame me for what occurred at trial.

If you are living through a high conflict divorce, own a business, maybe even dealing with a narcissist/abuser and have children, Peter is THE man to get you through it.  Nothing, and I mean nothing, rattles this guy.  Cool, calm, collected, he will literally destroy the other side and eat them for lunch.  He has a way of settling you down when you’re coming unglued.  Fees were fair, especially for the complicated nightmare he fought through.  Side note: as we chatted on the phone one evening while I was in turmoil after hours, I heard his snarling Rottweiler in the background.  He casually said “oh, he thinks he owns the canal and doesn’t want anyone coming by”.  I laughed and said “he sounds like the perfect dog for you…you guys have matching personalities”.  Peter is known as a “formidable foe” in the legal world, and I would completely agree.  Communication could be improve, but in the end he still crushes it.  His assistant Lucyana is a doll, helpful with a great sense of humor.  Everyone in the firm knew about my crazy case.  Pete’s wife Cathy was fantastic.  Jose was AMAZING, calm, grounded, ACTUALLY LISTENED and retained a ton of information even 2 years after being told, handled much of my case, articulates details absolutely perfectly in court documents, took several of my calls and gave me sound direction.  Even Lisa at the front desk was sweet and very helpful.  You just can’t go wrong here.  But if you need a bulldog, (or Rottweiler) Peter is your guy.

Diane and I have had multiple opportunities to work with T.R. Smith, a partner in the Mackey Law Group. The courtesy and care from the front desk to the paralegals has been exemplary and T.R. and his team were never more than a phone call away. Our interactions with T.R. Smith were nothing short of exceptional. He acted as friendly advisor as well as attorney in a manner which was never condescending and always treated us with great respect. In both real estate and personal legal matters we knew our outcome would always achieve our greater good and we could depend upon T.R. to get things right the first time. I have only had one initial consultation with Peter Mackey and found him to be open to listening and quite positive in his direction. His level of experience-based confidence was most reassuring that a favorable outcome was possible in a seemingly endless and complicated legal tangle. I get the impression that if you are not an exceptional attorney you would not find a place at Mackey Law Group. Mackey Law Group with their client-first attitude will always receive our highest recommendations  when anyone we know is seeing the best in legal advice and outcomes.
Best lawyer in town
Peter Mackey and Jorge Martinez were great to work with.  Explaining the process of my divorce & guiding me through a difficult situation.
Staff here are always pleasant and professional and my lawyer is terrific!

When I was served papers for a custody dispute I didn’t really know how to proceed or where to turn. Kyle Stalnaker was recommended from a mom’s group I am part of on social media. He came highly recommended by many moms who have been in my same situation and he is known to “go to bat” for his clients and the children involved.
Throughout my time working with Kyle he was extremely professional, knowledgeable, and helpful for winning my case. He was able to sift through to the heart of what really mattered, and we strategized regularly about how best to proceed. Kyle was always informed and able to advise on best next steps in my case. One thing that really impressed me was how knowledgeable he was on my case. He always remembered the details that are so important when working with multiple clients and I could tell that he took my case seriously. For me, this was the most important thing. Kyle never lets his clients get lost in the shuffle and each case is important for him.
My case was very complicated and many times we had to decide on the best direction to move forward. Kyle always remained confident and truthful with where we were. He had a calming effect in a highly emotional situation and that was a relief. There were many lies, and misconceptions made by the other side and he was able to explain the legality of this and show me a way forward in navigating these hard problems.
I highly recommend Kyle Stalnaker for your divorce or custody suit and feel confident that he will have your best interest in mind.
Sarah Taylor

I had the misfortune of being in a serious accident when a motor vehicle ran a stop sign, causing a collision while riding my bicycle.  I suffered serious injuries and incurred 10s of thousands of dollars in medical bills.  Kyle Stalnaker of Mackey Law Group was recommended to me by a friend.  Kyle and his team including para-legal Shelley Waitkus worked tirelessly and relentlessly on my case.  They spent countless hours working with the insurance companies, medical providers, and the offender in order to see to it that my medical bills were covered, and I received just compensation for this accident.  This was not an 8-figure type case for their firm, yet they diligently fought for me every step of the way until a fair settlement was reached.  It is difficult to put into words just how grateful and privileged I feel to have had them on my side.  I would unequivocally recommend them to anyone in need of legal representation.  Thanks again Kyle, you really went above and beyond for me.

I recently enlisted the help of “General Contractor” to perform a bathroom/garage conversion on my home. I then found out that the permitting paperwork was never submitted, and the “contractor” was not licensed, to which he never disclosed this information to me. After requesting for termination of our contract, that was signed almost 2 months prior, I was met with resistance, anger, and threats of legal action.

Fortunately, I reached out to T.R. Smith who was extremely knowledgeable and thorough in reviewing the contract that I signed. He was able to demand a full refund of my initial deposit and termination of the contract.  T.R.‘s attention to detail and tenacity in doing what is right is like no other. This “contractor” took advantage of me and I’m grateful that T.R. was able to help conclude what could have been drawn out over time with no progress. This could have easily become an extremely unfortunate circumstance for me personally, especially knowing he is not the person who is licensed and insured, as advertised. I am grateful to have someone like T.R. on my side and in our community. I would highly recommend him!

He is diligent with investigating the truth and put in footwork to make sure I was treated fairly. We attended court through zoom due to the COVID-19. Kyle prepared me for every situation. As the situation came to a close another stunt was pulled by the other party and Kyle was able to quickly rectify it by getting a quick court date in to prevent it from going any further. I am grateful that I was led to Kyle Stalnaker to represent me. If anything should ever need to be handled in the future, I will always go to Kyle for help.

Whenever I am in need of a lawyers services or if I simply need some guidance in my business dealings, Kyle will be the one I ask.

reviews.ownerResponse: You have canceled multiple appointments. You were advised BEFORE you made the last appointment what the cost would be and you kept the appointment until an hour before you were supposed to be here and then called to cancel.  I have the email correspondence in which you were advised of the price back on Sept 24th, in response to your original email in early September.  You also called a couple times in the days leading up to the most recent appointment with questions and never bothered to cancel. Every time you cancel last minute, I have no opportunity to replace the time slot with someone else.  Additionally, when you called yesterday to set yet another appointment, you told my secretary that you were rescheduling because you had been out of town for the last appointment that you canceled. reviews.text:
Sierra Butler at Butler Elder Law is amazing! She’s kind, super knowledgeable, and really cares about helping families handle tough situations like planning for the future or protecting loved ones from elder fraud. She makes everything easy to understand and is there for you every step of the way.

We had Sierra on the Care Sandwich Podcast, and her passion for helping seniors and families really stood out. If you need someone who truly cares and will guide you through it all, reach out to Sierra—you’ll be glad you did!

At a time when I was overwhelmed with health, emotional, and financial issues Sierra and her staff were very supportive. They walked me through the processes I would require and made sure that I truly understood the information. The staff were helpful and supportive as well. I would highly recommend them as a guide through this difficult journey!

I can not rave about Sierra Butler at Butler Elder Law enough. Sierra helped my family and I through an extremely difficult time in our lives. We had two other lawyers who had neglected to do their jobs accurately and had failed to file important documents in a timely matter. I was panicking and stressed and Sierra came through in a big way. She literally fixed everything for us. We had a complicated situation and what started out as something we thought was going to take multiple court visits and multiple pleadings ended up being handled quickly and efficiently by Sierra. She was patient and kind and thorough in her explanations. She knew what she was talking about and after the first conversation with her, I knew we'd picked the right lawyer. She was always easily accessible, both on the phone and through email, and got back with me promptly. All of these are things we had not experienced with our other lawyers. I can say without a doubt that you will not be disappointed with her.

Butler Elder Law did a great job handling my fathers estate. They are extremely knowledgeable and made this so easy for me and we were in the height of covid and I live in New Mexico! Thank you Melissa for the great service!

The Butler Elder Law team, led by namesake Sierra, is knowledgeable, transparent, compassionate and works for you as if you were their own family. In this cynical time in history, it's good to know that you can still believe that good people are everywhere.
Sierra is a wonderfully caring empathetic and extremely knowledgeable elder care attorney. She is a pleasure to be around and her calm presence definitely helps when you need it most.
This law firm was extremely helpful in organizing our medical and financial forms!  I highly recommend them!
I'm very pleased with the support we received from Ms Butler and her office. She helped us navigate a difficult time with compassion and efficiency. Thanks!

When it became clear it was time to move my declining mother 100 miles closer to me in the Fort Myers area so I could better help manage her care, I was overwhelmed. Selling a home, a car, finding a good match in a facility, making the move, the legal aspects, these are not small things. I was all alone in this, and I didn't know how to proceed. A friend referred me to Sierra, and in only one meeting, she set me straight, pointed me in a good direction, and referred me to an amazing professional lady in my mom's area who, for a reasonable fee, got it done! This retired nurse arranged a realtor and sold the home, arranged the moving service and made the move, traveled 100 miles south to check out several living facilities, and found the one my mother is now settled.  Since the move and establishing new doctors under my supervision, my mother's meds have been adjusted, and 6 months later she is healthier and happier and much less confused. Consulting Sierra Butler Law was the exact right thing I needed to do. Sierra is smart, knowledgeable, ethical and honest, easy to talk to, and genuinely caring. And even though we are in different counties now,  when I need future Elder Law services, I will go out of my way to use Sierra Butler.

I've had the privilege of working with Cahall Law Firm for several years across multiple business entities, and the level of service, integrity, and expertise they've provided has been consistently outstanding. Kristina has been our primary point of contact, and I can’t speak highly enough about her professionalism and dedication.

She has handled a wide range of legal matters for us, including corporate governance, contract drafting and review, business litigation, lien enforcement, and general legal strategy. No matter how complex the issue, Kristina approaches it with a deep understanding of the law, a practical mindset, and a calm, confident demeanor that instills trust.

What really sets her apart is her communication. She always makes sure we fully understand our options, including the potential risks and benefits, before moving forward. Her advice is clear, actionable, and tailored to the unique needs of each of our companies.

Just as importantly, we've never once felt overbilled or misled—everything is handled with complete transparency. In an industry where trust is everything, Cahall Law Firm and Kristina have more than earned ours.

If you're looking for a legal partner who is not only sharp and reliable but also genuinely cares about doing what’s right for your business, I couldn’t recommend Kristina and the entire Cahall team more highly.

Kris "Cowboy" at Leendorf Major Holding Company

My typical reviews are of things that truly don't matter, and thus my scale being from 1 to 1, with a 1 showing that I found everything satisfactory for the price paid. A burger at Wendys garnering the same acclaim as a high priced dry aged steak. Not because the two are equal, but because the standards for a $100 meal are far higher and therefore harder to meet than a $1.99 Jr. Bacon Cheeseburger.

Were my experience with the Czaia Law team to be held to such a sliding scale, they still would have easily cleared that bar despite their proverbial cheeseburger being more expensive than my first house.

From my first call through to the last drop of ink they were caring, kind, and professional. Be it taking the time to explain various legal intricacies, tempering expectations, guiding me through a tangled web of medical chaos, and getting visibly angry on my behalf, they were what I needed by my side.

Vanessa was simply amazing at coordianting a littany of issues as they arose. From Doctors being stupid, to other lawyers being stupid, to me being stupid. Let's be honest, it was mostly me. Vanessa wrangled it all with an expert grasp.

And then there was CJ. The man, the myth, the legend on the billboard. My case was a rollercoaster and he was constanly working to keep my car on the track despite every attempt from the opposition and myself to add more loops mid journey.

The Czaia Law firm receives my coveted one of one star. I would litigate here.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your generous review! We are beyond grateful for your trust in Czaia Law. It's wonderful to hear that our team, especially Vanessa and Mr. Czaia, were able to guide you through what sounds like quite the challenging journey. Your satisfaction means the world to us, and we are thrilled that we could be there for you every step of the way. If you ever need assistance again in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out. Thanks again for choosing us!

reviews.text: If i can give zero out of zero i would, “Do NOT I REPEAT DO NOT SIGN there settlement paper without checking all of there payments receipts that have been sent”, they do not pay all your bills, worst part about it they make you wait weeks or even months in my situation  to get ahold of them for anything you need to pay off collections, there reviews for there business im sure are friends and relatives or other business friends. Do not come to this business without full knowledge of how the legal system works or they will most definitely take full advantage of you.
reviews.ownerResponse: Czaia Law prides itself in achieving maximized settlements for our clients, as we did for you.  You received the entire policy limits that were available in your case.  Not only did we make sure that you received the full amount available, we also negotiated your bills with each provider, reducing the amount you owed, maximizing the amount of funds disbursed to you.  Each provider was paid, and proof of that was provided to you upon your request.  Any questions and/or concerns were addressed during your multiple visits to our office, whether impromptu or scheduled, and we answered each of your phone calls immediately.  For over thirty (30) years, I have worked hard to ensure that each client receives personal care from my office and that they are justly compensated for their injuries. However, our hands are often tied if there is either no insurance or not enough insurance.  Florida is often called the wild, wild, west state when it comes to insurance. If you do not carry adequate coverage on your own policy, do not be surprised when you do not get an adequate settlement.  Your recovery is based on many different factors including fault, injury, and coverage.  Florida’s “full coverage” does not require Bodily Injury (BI) or Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist (UM/UIM) insurance.  In your particular situation, had you purchased a higher amount of UM/UIM on your policy, your results would have been much greater.  We value each review and opinion, whether it comes from a client, family member, friend, and/or business.  Should you wish to further discuss this matter in detail, please contact our office directly at 833-255-6697.

Family law is a very sensitive matter built on several emotions. When selecting an attorney look for years of practice, fee amounts, bar complaints and posted reviews. Where there is smoke most likely there is fire.  I have been assigned to family law for over 9 years now, I have seen Mr. Peak practice in family law.  As someone in the court room and behind the scenes believe me you need an attorney that is well respected by the personnel in the court room who maybe deciding your case.  As a client you will not know that unless you listen to this review.

If you think throwing large amounts of money in the case with an attorney who charges a lot or an attorney who promises to file motions after motions ( fee driven), you maybe disappointed in the outcome.  You need a level headed, honest attorney that tells you the possible true outcomes. When an attorney cannot give you level headed advice along with fair answers to your questions that maybe a sign you are hiring the wrong person.

Mr. Peak is the opposite of those qualities, he is very honest, up front and forthcoming, you will see that when you meet him and  you will witness that in the court room.  I hope you follow my advice and good luck with your choices.

reviews.text: Losing a parent is never easy, especially when you live 1500 miles apart.  After dad passed there were a few loose financial ends that required us to probate the will. My step mom had suggested using an Attorney they had used in the past.  The number they quoted and the 3 to 6 month time frame seemed excessive.  I contacted my Attorney in NY and asked if she would provide me a short list of qualified estate attorneys in FL.  After phone interviewing several, I was compelled to use Worley Elder Law.  They answered the phone immediately, offered a flat rate and said they could probate the will in weeks.  Charles was concise, responsive, and professional leading to the best interaction I have ever had with a law firm.  Their actions matched their words, and their price was a fraction of other firms as well.  I would use Worley again in a heart beat and would recommend them to anyone.  They are fine people and do a good job.  Thank you Jami and Charles,  You made tough time in my life much easier and I appreciate it.  JR
reviews.ownerResponse: John, thank you for the kind review. We are pleased to hear that we met your expectations, and that we could alleviate some of the stress associated with probate.

reviews.text: I recently reached out to this business in regards to removing one name from a property deed. I was referred to a freshman lawyer at this firm. She was stuck in the mode of the due on sale clause involving mortgages and change in ownership. I explained clearly that I am not on the original mortgage. Also, I made clear that the one remaining individual on the deed is the original and only mortgage holder for the property. In fact, a local attorney practicing since 2001 and a graduate of Georgetown University advised me via email that the due on sale clause is not applicable to me. A second lawyer practicing Real Estate since 2008 advised me via email stating that I am not on the mortgage, the clause is not applicable to my situation. Of course, I presented proof of this email exchange to this business, as expected, they did not respond. Out of 57 Google reviews, the business has a total of 19% negative reviews (a total of 11 negative reviews).

Save your time and choose a better law firm. They will not return your calls. The receptionist Kim will pick up the phone and hang up or just not answer your calls at all. Kim is very dismissive and rude. Very poor customer service when we are already dealing with a hard time.

I was nervous about the home closing process but the team patiently answered all of my many questions and explained all the documents in a way I could actually understand. I was never made to feel like just another file. They handled everything with such professionalism and care, which made the closing day go incredibly smoothly. I am so grateful for their help and would recommend them to anyone for a real estate closing.

Choosing an attorney is a very personal and one of the most important and expensive decisions you will ever make. I can only give my experience with this firm and hope it helps someone in making a decision.

Upon our first meeting with a partner and another attorney, we were advised they would take our case.   We were paired with an attorney who was fairly new and inexperienced in the type of case we had, unbeknownst to us. Communication was extremely poor and very few of our questions were answered, emails not responded to, and phone calls seldomly returned. We had done a lot of the background work and submitted documentation, and we were told by our attorney, “you’re making my job easier” and “you’ve done a lot of my work”, but after almost a year, we were no further along than when we started, but $10,000 poorer with very little answers to the valid questions we had. There were documents and interrogatories submitted to the court that we found ourselves by going to the Manatee County Court website that we were never told about or given to us by our attorney. We were always told by this attorney, “I’ll have to ask “so and so” to find out an answer to that question, only to never get our answer. It was only after a new partner started, eight months after we started, that we had some of our questions answered. By then, for us it was too late.  All through the process we were told by our attorney we had a strong case. In our opinion, by saying that, the firm was able to bill us more money to string us along. In disgust, we eventually dropped our case. We had very poor counsel and I would never use this firm again, nor would I recommend due to our experience with them. We take responsibility for not dropping them sooner, but that does not justify their poor service.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sincerely sorry that the experience of the Fabricius family with our firm was a source of frustration and did not meet the standard of service we are committed to providing.  The concerns  raised about communication were taken very seriously and acted upon decisively. Otherwise, the attorney-client privilege and the legal ethics rules prevent us from making any further statements, even if we disagree with the facts alleged or omitted.

I contracted with Barnes Walker to represent me in  a real estate problem and they totally let me down. I had been paying them on an hourly rate and they wrote one letter and then dropped the case.  The lawyer I was working with took the case then in about a month went on maternity leave and left me hanging.  She should have never taken the case because I am sure she had some indication that she was pregnant and would not be available to finish and they had no one to fill in for her.  She indicated that she was having a phone call to open up negotiations and she would call me the next day.  Still waiting for that call which should have taken place 3 weeks ago.
Outstanding explanation of every aspect of the closing process. I will be back.

I am very pleased with their work. We closed on our home today. Jamie Robertson and our Agent Rhonda De Polo from KW were  super nice and the whole thing went flawlessly. I can only recommend them and thank you so much to the both of you.
Stefan & Tina Heidkamp
Excellent service and quality work. Staff is very professional.
They handled the closing on our home and did a great job. Very professional.

Always polite professional And care about my situation. Good people.😊
Great place. Professional firm with nice helpful people to help you get through the home buying process. Highly recommend.

This firm cost me an extra $495 and untold STRESS  I had to pay my moving company due to Kara & one of the law partners not calling in to start wiring my funds until 1:50 PM. When we had our request in at 11:00 AM.   We where assured  (3 days prior to closings at time of signing)  that the minute our call was made that we had vacated our old house the wire transfer would begin so we could access our new home as quickly as possible. When I tried to speak to someone at the firm that we where out at 11:00 AM I could not reach anyone and kept leaving messages to start funds transfer and get back to me that they had indeed started as promised. Two trucks with 4 movers sat in the road waiting to get access to our new home for over 3 hours.  With the long delay of fund transfer, which once the call was made by the attorney only took 1hour & 15 min. to arrive we didn't get access to our new home till 3:15 ! We scrambled to get the trucks unloaded and none of my furniture or belongings got put together or deposited in the right room.
The stress was overwhelming as our movers threatened to take our belongs to some storage facility at our cost and would not be able to continue to move us for another week.
I have had well over 20 closings in my time and I have never come across a firm so negligent.  Be it a Title Company or another Attorney no issues like this.  This firm was unfortunately hired by the buyers of our house, not us.  Be forewarned to not hire them if you want a smooth transition.
Excellent law firm, very knowledgeable and efficient. Highly recommend if you need a lawyer for estate planning.
Poor customer service and not professional!

I am a retired personal injury lawyer and a retired Judge from New York. During my 40 years of practicing law; I have been involved with or presided over thousands of personal injury cases. As you can imagine; I had many choices available to me (in selecting a lawyer) when I was I struck by another vehicle while walking in a parking lot.  As a result of the offending driver’s negligence: I was seriously injured and required spinal surgery.

After a great deal of research and several interviews, I had the privilege of meeting Carl Reynolds. From the outset, Mr. Reynolds was engaging and extremely professional.

He and his team, especially Brett, were involved and engaged in my lawsuit.  I was injured on 12/4/2024 and within one year; my case was settled for not one policy but they were able to secure additional compensation from a secondary policy.  They also, took care of my health care bills and made sure that all of the bills were paid and that I would not be left holding the “bag” so to speak  with surprise bills from health care providers after my case was resolved.  This latter point is critical when selecting a personal injury lawyer.

I highly recommend this firm. Mr. Reynolds is an experienced, first rate lawyer and an engaging and compassionate person.  Thank you Mr. Reynolds and team for a wonderful job.  I’m grateful.  The Judge.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Good Morning Judge,
Thank you so much for taking the time to write us a review. It means the world to us to be able to serve and help those in need--especially one as esteemed as yourself with knowledge of and experience in personal injury law and jurisprudence. We know you had a lot of options out there and we are humbled and grateful you gave us the opportunity to serve you. It was truly our pleasure! We wish you all the best in all your future endeavors. Don't be a stranger!
Best Regards,
Carl Reynolds, Owner

Update: This firm owner appreciates an opportunity to become better. The down-to-earth accountability and understanding that nobody's perfect is expressed honestly by him.  His Kind response shows sensitivity with understanding that mistakes happen.
reviews.ownerResponse: Good Evening.  I am so, so sorry for your negative interaction with my firm recently and that your recent experience is completely out of character with your prior experiences with our firm and the experience of a vast majority of our clients.  We have four offices along the Suncoast; so, we are usually less than 45 minutes away from most Manatee and Sarasota County residents--I'm sorry we do not have an office location closer to where you live.  In addition, while our normal operating hours are 8am to 5pm, there is almost always an attorney or paralegal working at our four locations at hours prior to and well after normal business hours.  Please feel free to call me directly so I can figure out how this could have happened in this very isolated instance and to make sure it never happens again.  Thank you for bringing this issue to my attention so that we can improve on our standard of care to all our precious clients and potential clients.  Thank you.

reviews.text:
They say ‘injuries are personal’ but they will choose who they want to ‘fight’ for or against and for what reason. They may make a few calls, but they don’t want to have to ‘fight’ for you. They let my daughter’s case go after the pip insurance ran out when it was against the city of Sarasota and the police dept from an ambulance that ran a red light and crashed into my truck she was driving. They say they had reason not to pursue, but it was not even a justifiable contributing factor in the case. Just a good excuse not to continue the case.
Don’t believe they’re not in it for the easy money, they’ll get the FIRST third of anything they get for you. But I’m sure this is true for all the injury attorneys. Caveat emptor-let the buyer beware or aware. We were referred to  them via the physical therapist that was providing the care.
reviews.ownerResponse: Good Evening and thank you for your feedback.  Please have your daughter call me directly so I can explain further the issue that prevented us from moving forward on her case.  As you may or may not know, the Florida legislature passed a sweeping tort reform law back in March 2023 that renders certain claims under certain circumstances no longer prosecutable.  It has nothing to do with "easy money" versus hard fought money--it has to do with what the law allows as compensable injuries and what the law no longer allows.  We do not shy away from difficult cases or cases involving a "fight."  In fact, earlier this year we obtained a $2,400,000 verdict against Sarasota County--a case Sarasota County denied and fought us all the way through a weeklong trial until a jury of our client's peers awarded our permanently injured client $2.4MM.  We are always fighting against big bully insurance companies and corporations (and even the government if it is justified) on behalf of our wrongfully injured clients--and we will always do so--whatever it takes within the confines of the law.
Thank you Emmanuel for your review!  It was our pleasure serving you!

reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Jones, I represented you for a DUI in Manatee County. The case number is 2023 CT 920. From the first time we spoke, and you retained my services, everything out of your mouth was an utter lie. You initially told me you never had a DUI and this was a first offense. Only after I made efforts to help you obtain a hardship driver's license by contacting DHSMV did I learn you have at least four prior DUI's on your record. The State Attorney's Office also made me aware of your criminal driving history. I telephoned you to discuss this, and you told me it wasn't important that I knew about the prior DUI's, so you intentionally concealed this information from me.

You refused to come to my office to discuss the facts of your case, but absolutely insisted on going to trial instead of listening to my advice. The reason why you lost at trial was due to a number of reasons. First, the police video recorded the entire encounter lasting approximately an hour. The video had you passed out in the McDonald's drive thru on SR 70 at 2am, with the engine running and the radio blaring. Second, once the initial responding deputy woke you up, you created a hostile interaction between the four other responding police officers. This was all captured on each of their body cameras. Third, your behavior in the courtroom was simply bizarre. You would not verbally respond to the judge when he asked you a question. It was obvious the jury strongly disliked you. Quite honestly, you're an angry man with a chip on his shoulder. The worst part about your case was that you are/were a Manatee County educator and tried to conceal this arrest from your supervisors.

Huge thanks to Mrs. Christina Goldberg whom I had a luck to represent my interests in the divorce case after I fired my previous attorney who dragged it over for 2.5 years with absolutely no results.
I got recommendation for this office from a stranger, a lady on my neighborhood app after I ask the community member for help to find a reliable attorney. The woman said to me "Hire this attorney, she will fight for you". Today I only wish I found Mrs. Goldberg before my painful legal journey.

I want to tell you, that I met/considered/interviewed a significant number of the attorneys in the area and found many of them lacking compassion, or knowledge, or being errogant, even rude. The two that I hired were - one simply got his money to be the mediator, other - dragged my case for two and half years with no results and was terrible communicator and a liar.
There was a different vibe with Lurhsen & Goldberg office: a straightforward communication, no empty promises, punctuality, and professionalism. I did not need to wander for months what was going on with my case: I was updated in the timely manner. Their paralegals are great too.
At the end, I even got a refund from my deposit and was charged for the actual work.
If you are looking for a professional, honest, and a reliable legal representation, look no further.
I plan to come back to their office to take care of my estate and living will, to ensure that my son would be legally protected in case of my death.

Fighting for Justice: A Grateful Family's Experience

Our case involved a claim of negligence against a former daycare regarding the well-being of our son. Throughout this challenging experience, Mr. Podor and his team proved to be invaluable allies in our pursuit of justice.
